headline: The recommended educators converge on orchestrating and governing multiple coding agents as the central engineering problem this cycle.
body: Cole Medin presents Omnigent, a meta-harness that routes tasks across Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers with human-in-the-loop governance and cross-device sessions [2], while Cyril Imhof frames harness engineering and loops as the way to build AI applications for regulated industries, weighing open versus closed models and model shutdown risk [0]. IBM Technology supplies the surrounding infrastructure arguments: Kagenti secures multi-agent systems with identity-based controls and delegation chains [1], smart routing is replacing raw model size as labs chase cost and trustworthiness [5], and MCP plus design systems guide agents toward consistent code [3][6]. On the systems side, Better Stack stress tests Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model, which exceeds 1,000 tokens per second using FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel runtime [7], and Google Cloud Tech demonstrates long-running, event-driven agents with the open source ADK [10][13].
